app.title: N3UR0N
workspace.hint.network: Select a peer from the list to inspect its details.
workspace.hint.skills: Select a skill from the list to inspect its manifest.
files.col.name: Name / MIME
files.subtitle: Your uploaded and received blobs.
files.empty.body: Upload a file to stage it locally (class D), or pick another blob class in the sidebar.
sidebar.files.empty: no files yet — upload to stage for invoke
sidebar.files.deleteConfirm: Delete this file from local storage?
settings.title: Settings
settings.planner.title: Planner model
settings.planner.subtitle: Choose which LLM compiles plans and composes replies. The server default is the minimum bootstrap model.
settings.planner.disabled: No planner is configured on this node. Set N3UR0N_PLANNER_MODE when starting the server.
settings.planner.model.help: Leave empty to use the backend's default model.
settings.planner.no_backends: Add an openai_compat backend under Backends to unlock stronger models.
settings.ui.title: Interface
settings.ui.subtitle: Tune how the app looks and feels. Settings persist per browser / Tauri install.
settings.ui.language.help: UI language. Drop a JSON file in ui/locales/ to add a new one.
settings.ui.theme.help: Color scheme. System follows your OS preference.
settings.backends.title: Backends
settings.backends.subtitle: Where AI calls go. Add a local LLM (Ollama), a cloud API (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral), or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
settings.backends.empty.title: No backends yet
settings.backends.empty.body: A backend tells N3UR0N where to send AI calls — your local Ollama, an OpenAI key, an MCP server, or any HTTP API. Add your first one to get started.
settings.caps.title: Skills
settings.caps.subtitle: Capabilities (skills) declared in your manifests. They're invokable by this client and can be shared with peers.
settings.caps.empty.title: No skills yet
settings.caps.empty.body: A skill is a capability you expose: a translation, a search, a structured chat. Use the composer to declare your first one.
settings.gateways.title: Gateways
settings.gateways.subtitle: Remote n3ur0n peers you've added. Their skills appear in your catalog.
settings.gateways.add_help: Pull describe_self from a remote n3ur0n endpoint and add it to your peer directory. If you have no gateways yet, use the public seed.
settings.gateways.empty.title: No gateways yet
settings.gateways.empty.body: A gateway is a remote n3ur0n peer. Add one to discover skills — or use the public seed seed.n3ur0n.net.
settings.gateways.already_added: This gateway is already in your list
settings.gateways.public_already: Public seed is already added as a gateway
settings.gateways.seeder.label: Startup seed
settings.gateways.unseed_confirm: Stop using {endpoint} as a startup seed? It stays in your gateway list.
settings.gateways.delete_confirm: Remove {endpoint} from your gateway directory? Seed status is unchanged unless you remove it from seeds separately.
settings.about.language.help: UI language. Drop a JSON file in ui/locales/ to add a new one.
composer.placeholder: Type your message and press Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)…
composer.mode.direct.tooltip: Skip planning — one LLM reply per message (uses the planner model).
conv.empty_hint: Pick a conversation in the sidebar or click + New chat.
backend.form.intro.create: Declare a backend: an LLM endpoint, an MCP server, or an HTTP API base. Written to backends/<name>.toml. Hot-reloaded — no restart required.
backend.form.intro.edit: Edit the backend manifest. Changes hot-reload — no restart required. Capability bindings referencing this backend are rebuilt automatically.
backend.form.name: name
backend.form.kind.locked: Changing kind is not supported — delete and re-create instead.
backend.form.openai.title: openai_compat config
backend.form.openai.base_url.help: Host only — e.g. http://192.168.4.101:11434 for Ollama. Do not add /api/generate or /v1.
(leave empty to keep the existing key)
backend.form.mcp.title: mcp_server config
backend.form.http.title: http_base config
backend.form.http.headers: default headers (Key: value, one per line)
backend.form.delete_confirm: Delete backend "{name}"? Capabilities referencing it will fail to invoke until rewired.
cap.form.field.name: name
cap.form.field.mode.tooltip: Public: any signed peer can invoke. Restricted: peers must present a subscription token. Private: local-only, never advertised or invokable over the network.
cap.form.field.disambig.placeholder: When to prefer this skill vs others. When NOT to use it.
cap.form.field.binding.locked: changing binding type on an existing cap is not supported — delete and re-create instead
cap.form.field.http.headers: extra headers (Key: value, one per line)
cap.form.field.mcp.arg_mapping: arg_mapping (JSON, maps incoming args to MCP tool input)
cap.form.field.mcp.result_mapping: result_mapping (JSON, maps MCP result to schema_out)
cap.template.picker.title: New skill — pick a starting point
cap.template.picker.intro: Pick a template to prefill the form — every field stays editable — or start from a blank skill.
cap.template.blank.summary: Start from scratch. No fields prefilled beyond minimal defaults.
filter.type.tooltip: Filter by access mode or binding type
auth.login.title: Sign in
auth.bootstrap.title: Create the first admin
auth.bootstrap.help: Nobody has registered yet. Pick a username + password for the first administrator. You can add more users from Settings.
settings.users.title: Users
settings.users.subtitle: Create new accounts, assign roles, reset passwords.
settings.users.empty.title: Only you so far
settings.users.empty.body: Add operators or fellow admins to share this publisher.
